web_text: The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham. Abraham became the father of Isaac. Isaac became the father of Jacob. Jacob became the father of Judah and his brothers. Judah became the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar. Perez became the father of Hezron. Hezron became the father of Ram. Ram became the father of Amminadab. Amminadab became the father of Nahshon. Nahshon became the father of Salmon.
slang_text: Okay, so here's the family tree, totally for real -- this is Jesus Christ's roots, David's line, going all the way back to Abraham. Abraham had a kid named Isaac. Isaac cranked out Jacob. Jacob had Judah and his bros. Judah and Tamar hooked up and had Perez and Zerah. Perez had Hezron. Hezron had Ram. Ram had Amminadab. Amminadab had Nahshon. Nahshon had Salmon. Righteous lineup so far, dudes.

web_text: Salmon became the father of Boaz by Rahab. Boaz became the father of Obed by Ruth. Obed became the father of Jesse. Jesse became the father of King David. David became the father of Solomon by her who had been Uriah’s wife. Solomon became the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam became the father of Abijah. Abijah became the father of Asa. Asa became the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat became the father of Joram. Joram became the father of Uzziah.
slang_text: Salmon and Rahab had Boaz. Boaz and Ruth had Obed. Obed had Jesse. Jesse had King David -- fer sure a big deal, that guy. David and Uriah's old lady had Solomon (bogus way that went down, but no duh it's still part of the story). Solomon had Rehoboam. Rehoboam had Abijah. Abijah had Asa. Asa had Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat had Joram. Joram had Uzziah. Party on, the line keeps rolling.

web_text: Uzziah became the father of Jotham. Jotham became the father of Ahaz. Ahaz became the father of Hezekiah. Hezekiah became the father of Manasseh. Manasseh became the father of Amon. Amon became the father of Josiah. Josiah became the father of Jechoniah and his brothers, at the time of the exile to Babylon. After the exile to Babylon, Jechoniah became the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel became the father of Zerubbabel.
slang_text: Uzziah had Jotham. Jotham had Ahaz. Ahaz had Hezekiah. Hezekiah had Manasseh. Manasseh had Amon. Amon had Josiah. Josiah had Jechoniah and his bros, right around when everybody got dragged off to Babylon -- way harsh exile, total bummer. After that whole mess, Jechoniah had Shealtiel. Shealtiel had Zerubbabel.

web_text: Zerubbabel became the father of Abiud. Abiud became the father of Eliakim. Eliakim became the father of Azor. Azor became the father of Zadok. Zadok became the father of Achim. Achim became the father of Eliud. Eliud became the father of Eleazar. Eleazar became the father of Matthan. Matthan became the father of Jacob. Jacob became the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, from whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ.
slang_text: Zerubbabel had Abiud. Abiud had Eliakim. Eliakim had Azor. Azor had Zadok. Zadok had Achim. Achim had Eliud. Eliud had Eleazar. Eleazar had Matthan. Matthan had Jacob. And Jacob had Joseph -- yeah, that Joseph, the guy who married Mary. And Mary's the one who had Jesus, the dude they call Christ. Cowabunga, we made it to the main event.

web_text: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; from David to the exile to Babylon fourteen generations; and from the carrying away to Babylon to the Christ, fourteen generations. Now the birth of Jesus Christ was like this: After his mother, Mary, was engaged to Joseph, before they came together, she was found pregnant by the Holy Spirit. Joseph, her husband, being a righteous man, and not willing to make her a public example, intended to put her away secretly. But when he thought about these things, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, “Joseph, son of David, don’t be afraid to take to yourself Mary, your wife, for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Spirit.
slang_text: So check the math: from Abraham to David, fourteen generations, fer sure. From David to the Babylon exile, fourteen more. From that exile to the Christ, fourteen more -- three sets of fourteen, no lie. Now here's how Jesus Christ's birth totally went down: his mom Mary was engaged to Joseph, but before they'd even gotten together, boom -- she turns up pregnant, and it's straight-up from the Holy Spirit. Joseph, being a stand-up guy and not wanting to make a big bogus scene out of it, figured he'd quietly call off the engagement instead of humiliating her. But while he's mulling all this over, an angel of the Lord rolls up in a dream and says, "Joseph, son of David, don't sweat it, dude -- go ahead and take Mary as your wife, 'cause what's growing in her is from the Holy Spirit, for real."

"She's gonna have a son. Name him Jesus, 'cause he's the one who's gonna save his people from their sins." All this went down so it'd line up with what the Lord said through the prophet, way back when:
"Check it, the virgin's gonna be pregnant,
and she'll have a son,
and they're gonna call him Immanuel" --
which means, translated for us regular folks, "God's with us." Fer sure. So Joseph wakes up from his sleep and does exactly what the angel told him -- no arguing, no trippin' -- and takes Mary as his wife.

web_text: and didn’t know her sexually until she had given birth to her firstborn son. He named him Jesus.
slang_text: but he didn't sleep with her, kept it totally chill, until she'd had her firstborn son. And he named him Jesus, just like he was told. Righteous.

web_text: Now when Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of King Herod, behold, wise men from the east came to Jerusalem, saying, “Where is he who is born King of the Jews? For we saw his star in the east, and have come to worship him.” When King Herod heard it, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him. Gathering together all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he asked them where the Christ would be born.
slang_text: So Jesus got born in Bethlehem, over in Judea, back when King Herod was runnin' the show. Check it -- some totally tubular wise dudes rolled in from the east, straight into Jerusalem, askin' around, "Yo, where's this kid who's born King of the Jews? We spotted his star way out east and we bounced all the way here just to worship him, for real." When Herod heard that news it was way harsh -- dude got seriously freaked, and the whole city of Jerusalem was trippin' right along with him. So he rounds up all the head priests and the scribes and grills 'em on where this Christ was even supposed to show up.

web_text:
They said to him, “In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is written through the prophet, ‘You Bethlehem, land of Judah,
are in no way least among the princes of Judah:
for out of you shall come a governor,
who shall shepherd my people, Israel.’” Then Herod secretly called the wise men, and learned from them exactly what time the star appeared. He sent them to Bethlehem, and said, “Go and search diligently for the young child. When you have found him, bring me word, so that I also may come and worship him.”
slang_text: They gave it to him straight: "In Bethlehem of Judea, dude -- it's written right there by the prophet: 'You, Bethlehem, land of Judah, you ain't no bogus nobody among the princes of Judah -- outta you's gonna come a governor who's gonna shepherd my people Israel.'" So Herod gets the wise men together on the down-low, pumps 'em for the exact time that star popped up. Then he ships 'em off to Bethlehem, tellin' 'em, "Go dig around real careful for this little kid. Soon as you find him, bring me the word, so I can bounce over there and worship him too." (I'm so sure, dude -- what's your damage, Herod, that ain't your real plan.)

web_text: They, having heard the king, went their way; and behold, the star, which they saw in the east, went before them, until it came and stood over where the young child was. When they saw the star, they rejoiced with exceedingly great joy. They came into the house and saw the young child with Mary, his mother, and they fell down and worshiped him. Opening their treasures, they offered to him gifts: gold, frankincense, and myrrh. Being warned in a dream that they shouldn’t return to Herod, they went back to their own country another way.
slang_text: After they heard the king out, they hit the road again, and check this -- that star they'd seen way out east was cruisin' right in front of 'em, till it stopped and hovered right over the spot where the little kid was chillin'. When they saw that star, man, they were stoked beyond stoked, like majorly, epically psyched. They rolled into the house and saw the little kid there with Mary, his mom, and they dropped down and worshiped him, no joke. They busted open their treasure chests and laid some bodacious gifts on him -- gold, frankincense, and myrrh. Then, gettin' a heads-up in a dream not to swing back by Herod's place, they bounced home to their own country a whole different way.

web_text: Now when they had departed, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream, saying, “Arise and take the young child and his mother, and flee into Egypt, and stay there until I tell you, for Herod will seek the young child to destroy him.” He arose and took the young child and his mother by night, and departed into Egypt, and was there until the death of Herod; that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying, “Out of Egypt I called my son.” Then Herod, when he saw that he was mocked by the wise men, was exceedingly angry, and sent out, and killed all the male children who were in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding countryside, from two years old and under, according to the exact time which he had learned from the wise men.
slang_text: Soon as they'd bounced, check it -- an angel of the Lord shows up to Joseph in a dream, sayin', "Get up, grab the little kid and his mom, and book it to Egypt, and stay put there till I tell you different, 'cause Herod's gonna hunt this kid down to waste him." So Joseph got up, grabbed the kid and his mom in the middle of the night, and took off for Egypt, stayin' there till Herod kicked it -- so that what the Lord said through the prophet would come true: "Outta Egypt I called my son." Meanwhile Herod, once he figured out the wise men had played him, went totally ballistic. This part ain't no joke, no punchline here -- he sent out his men and slaughtered every baby boy in Bethlehem and the whole surrounding area, two years old and under, matchin' up the exact time he'd squeezed outta the wise men. Straight-up evil, way beyond bogus.

web_text:
Then that which was spoken by Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled, saying, “A voice was heard in Ramah,
lamentation, weeping and great mourning,
Rachel weeping for her children;
she wouldn’t be comforted,
because they are no more.” But when Herod was dead, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt, saying, “Arise and take the young child and his mother, and go into the land of Israel, for those who sought the young child’s life are dead.”
slang_text: Then what Jeremiah the prophet had said came true, for real: "A voice got heard in Ramah, straight-up wailin' and cryin' and heavy mournin' -- Rachel weepin' for her kids, and she wouldn't take no chill pill, 'cause they were just gone." Way harsh stuff. But once Herod finally kicked the bucket, an angel of the Lord shows up in a dream to Joseph, over there in Egypt, sayin', "Get up, grab the little kid and his mom, and head on back to the land of Israel -- the dudes who wanted this kid dead are dead themselves now."

web_text: He arose and took the young child and his mother, and came into the land of Israel. But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in the place of his father, Herod, he was afraid to go there. Being warned in a dream, he withdrew into the region of Galilee, and came and lived in a city called Nazareth; that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophets: “He will be called a Nazarene.”
slang_text: So Joseph got up, grabbed the little kid and his mom, and rolled into the land of Israel. But when he heard that Archelaus was runnin' Judea now, takin' over for his dad Herod, he got spooked about goin' there. So gettin' another heads-up in a dream, he veered off into the region of Galilee instead, and ended up settlin' down in a town called Nazareth -- so that what got spoken through the prophets would come true: "He's gonna be called a Nazarene." Fer sure, that's how it went down.

web_text:
In those days, John the Baptizer came, preaching in the wilderness of Judea, saying, “Repent, for the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand!” For this is he who was spoken of by Isaiah the prophet, saying,
“The voice of one crying in the wilderness,
make ready the way of the Lord.
Make his paths straight.” Now John himself wore clothing made of camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist. His food was locusts and wild honey.
slang_text:
Back in those days, this dude John the Baptizer rolls up, preaching out in the Judea wilderness, hollerin', "Get right, 'cause the Kingdom of Heaven is totally at hand!" This is the guy Isaiah the prophet was talkin' about way back when, sayin':

"There's a voice hollerin' out in the wilderness,
get the Lord's road ready.
Make his paths straight." Now John himself was rockin' clothes made outta camel hair, with a leather belt cinched around his waist. Dude's whole diet was locusts and wild honey -- grody, but hey, it worked for him.

web_text: Then people from Jerusalem, all of Judea, and all the region around the Jordan went out to him. They were baptized by him in the Jordan, confessing their sins. But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming for his baptism, he said to them, “You offspring of vipers, who warned you to flee from the wrath to come? Therefore produce fruit worthy of repentance!
slang_text: So then, people from Jerusalem, all over Judea, and everybody up and down the Jordan region bounced out to see him. He baptized 'em right there in the Jordan while they fessed up their sins, fer sure. But when he clocked a bunch of Pharisees and Sadducees showin' up for baptism too, he laid into 'em: "You bunch of snake spawn, who tipped you off to bail from the wrath that's comin'? Quit trippin' and actually produce some fruit that proves you've changed!"

web_text: Don’t think to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham for our father,’ for I tell you that God is able to raise up children to Abraham from these stones. “Even now the ax lies at the root of the trees. Therefore every tree that doesn’t produce good fruit is cut down, and cast into the fire. I indeed baptize you in water for repentance, but he who comes after me is mightier than I, whose shoes I am not worthy to carry. He will baptize you in the Holy Spirit. His winnowing fork is in his hand, and he will thoroughly cleanse his threshing floor. He will gather his wheat into the barn, but the chaff he will burn up with unquenchable fire.”
slang_text: "And don't you dare go thinkin' to yourselves, 'We got Abraham for our pops, so we're covered.' I'm tellin' you fer real, God could raise up kids for Abraham outta these stones layin' right here, no duh. The ax is already restin' at the root of the trees, dudes. Any tree that ain't crankin' out good fruit gets chopped down and tossed in the fire, bogus as that sounds. I'm baptizin' you with water for gettin' right, but the one comin' after me is way more major than I am -- I ain't even worthy to carry his sandals, that's how it is. He's gonna baptize you with the Holy Spirit. He's got his winnowing fork in hand, and he's gonna clean out his threshing floor like a boss. He'll gather up his wheat into the barn, but the chaff? That's goin' up in fire that never quits burnin'."

web_text:
Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to John, to be baptized by him. But John would have hindered him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and you come to me?” But Jesus, answering, said to him,
“Allow it now, for this is the fitting way for us to fulfill all righteousness.” Then he allowed him. Jesus, when he was baptized, went up directly from the water: and behold, the heavens were opened to him. He saw the Spirit of God descending as a dove, and coming on him.
slang_text: Then Jesus rolls in from Galilee to the Jordan, headin' straight for John, wantin' to get baptized by him. But John tried to talk him outta it, sayin', "Whoa, hold up -- I'm the one who needs baptizin' by you, and you're comin' to me?" But Jesus answers him, chill but firm, "Let it happen for now, 'cause this is the righteous way for us to make good on everything that's right." So John let him. And soon as Jesus got baptized, he came straight up outta the water -- and check this out, the heavens totally cracked open right over him. He saw the Spirit of God comin' down like a dove, landin' right on him. Epic.

web_text: Behold, a voice out of the heavens said, “This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased.”
slang_text: And get a load of this -- a voice boomed outta the heavens, sayin', "This is my beloved Son, and dude, I am totally stoked on him."

web_text:
Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil. When he had fasted forty days and forty nights, he was hungry afterward. The tempter came and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command that these stones become bread.” But he answered,
“It is written, ‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceeds out of the mouth of God.’”
slang_text: So the Spirit hauled Jesus out into the wilderness, no joke, so the devil could take his best shot at him. Forty days and forty nights he didn't eat a thing, and by the end he was starving for real. The tempter rolls up and says, "If you're really God's Son, tell these rocks to turn into bread." But Jesus wasn't trippin' -- he shot back, "It's written: a dude can't live on bread alone. What feeds you for real is every word that comes outta God's mouth."

web_text:
Then the devil took him into the holy city. He set him on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down, for it is written, ‘He will put his angels in charge of you.’ and,
‘On their hands they will bear you up,
so that you don’t dash your foot against a stone.’” Jesus said to him,
“Again, it is written, ‘You shall not test the Lord, your God.’” Again, the devil took him to an exceedingly high mountain, and showed him all the kingdoms of the world, and their glory.
slang_text: Next the devil dragged him into the holy city and stood him up on the highest point of the temple. "If you're really God's Son," he says, "swan-dive off this thing -- it's written, 'He'll send his angels to catch you, they'll carry you in their hands so you don't even stub your toe on a stone.'" Jesus wasn't buying it. "It's also written," he said, "'don't test the Lord your God' -- that's bogus, and I'm not playing that game." So the devil hauls him up some seriously gnarly mountain and flashes him every kingdom on earth, all the glory that comes with 'em.

web_text:
He said to him, “I will give you all of these things, if you will fall down and worship me.” Then Jesus said to him,
“Get behind me,
Satan! For it is written, ‘You shall worship the Lord your God, and you shall serve him only.’” Then the devil left him, and behold, angels came and served him. Now when Jesus heard that John was delivered up, he withdrew into Galilee.
slang_text: "I'll hand over all of this," the devil says, "if you just drop down and worship me." Jesus had zero patience for it. "Bounce, Satan," he said, "get outta here -- it's written, 'You worship the Lord your God, period, him only, no side deals.'" That did it -- the devil took off, and angels showed up to look after Jesus, righteous and true. Then Jesus caught word that John got thrown in the slammer, so he cleared out and headed for Galilee.

web_text:
Leaving Nazareth, he came and lived in Capernaum, which is by the sea, in the region of Zebulun and Naphtali, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying, “The land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali,
toward the sea, beyond the Jordan,
Galilee of the Gentiles, the people who sat in darkness saw a great light,
to those who sat in the region and shadow of death,
to them light has dawned.”
slang_text: He split from Nazareth and set up shop in Capernaum, right by the sea, out in Zebulun and Naphtali territory -- exactly like the prophet Isaiah called it way back: "The land of Zebulun, the land of Naphtali, out toward the sea, past the Jordan, Galilee where all the Gentiles hang -- the people sitting in the dark caught a righteous light. For everybody stuck in the shadow of death, light finally dawned on 'em, fer sure."

web_text:
From that time, Jesus began to preach, and to say,
“Repent! For the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand.” Walking by the sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers: Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, his brother, casting a net into the sea; for they were fishermen. He said to them,
“Come after me, and I will make you fishers for men.” They immediately left their nets and followed him.
slang_text: From that point on, Jesus started preaching it loud: "Repent! The Kingdom of Heaven's totally at hand." Walking along the sea of Galilee, he spots two brothers, Simon (they call him Peter) and Andrew, chucking a net into the water -- fishermen, the real deal. He calls out to 'em, "Come hang with me, and I'll turn you into fishers of men, like a boss." No hesitation, dudes just dropped their nets right there and followed him.

web_text: Going on from there, he saw two other brothers, James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, in the boat with Zebedee their father, mending their nets. He called them. They immediately left the boat and their father, and followed him. Jesus went about in all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the Good News of the Kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness among the people. The report about him went out into all Syria. They brought to him all who were sick, afflicted with various diseases and torments, possessed with demons, epileptics, and paralytics; and he healed them.
slang_text: Moving on down the shore, he spots two more brothers, James and John, sons of Zebedee, out in the boat with their dad patching up nets. He calls 'em too, and just like that they bailed on the boat and their own father to tag along with Jesus -- no duh, this guy was worth following. Jesus cruised all over Galilee, teaching in the synagogues, spreading the epic Good News about the Kingdom, and healing every gnarly disease and sickness folks brought him. Word about him blew up all through Syria. People hauled in everybody who was sick, jacked up with all kinds of afflictions and torments, possessed by demons, seizing, paralyzed -- and he healed every last one, fer sure, straight up choice miracle work.

web_text: Great multitudes from Galilee, Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea and from beyond the Jordan followed him.
slang_text: Massive crowds started tailing him -- from Galilee, Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, even clear from past the Jordan. Dude had a whole posse now, for real.

So Jesus sees the crowds gettin' hella big, and He bounces up the mountain. Once He sits down, His crew rolls up next to Him, and He opens His mouth and starts droppin' the wisdom: "Totally blessed are the ones who know they're spiritually bankrupt, 'cause the Kingdom of Heaven is theirs, fer sure. Blessed are the ones who are cryin' their eyes out, 'cause they're gonna get comforted, no doubt."

Blessed are the chill, humble types -- they're gonna inherit the whole earth, for real. Blessed are the ones starvin' and dyin' of thirst for doin' right -- they're gonna get filled up like a boss. Blessed are the merciful -- mercy's comin' right back at 'em. And blessed are the ones with pure, clean hearts -- 'cause they're gonna see God Himself. Righteous.

Blessed are the peacemakers -- they're gettin' called God's own kids. Blessed are the ones catchin' grief for doin' the right thing -- Heaven's Kingdom is theirs, no lie. "Totally blessed are you when people talk trash on you, hassle you, and lie about you 'cause of Me. Don't have a cow -- party on, get stoked, 'cause your payoff up in heaven is epic. That's exactly how they treated the prophets way back before you, dude."

web_text: “You are the salt of the earth, but if the salt has lost its flavor, with what will it be salted? It is then good for nothing, but to be cast out and trodden under the feet of men. You are the light of the world. A city located on a hill can’t be hidden. Neither do you light a lamp, and put it under a measuring basket, but on a stand; and it shines to all who are in the house. Even so, let your light shine before men; that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father who is in heaven.
slang_text: "You dudes are the salt of the earth -- but if salt goes grody and loses its flavor, what's it good for? Nada. It's outta here, tossed and stomped on by everybody's feet. You're the light of the whole world. A city up on a hill can't hide, no way. Nobody lights a lamp and sticks it under a basket like some kinda dipstick -- you put it up on a stand so it shines on everybody in the house. So let your light shine in front of people, so they see your righteous deeds and give your Father in heaven the glory."

web_text:
“Don’t think that I came to destroy the law or the prophets. I didn’t come to destroy, but to fulfill. For most certainly, I tell you, until heaven and earth pass away, not even one smallest letter
or one tiny pen stroke
shall in any way pass away from the law, until all things are accomplished. Whoever, therefore, shall break one of these least commandments, and teach others to do so, shall be called least in the Kingdom of Heaven; but whoever shall do and teach them shall be called great in the Kingdom of Heaven. For I tell you that unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, there is no way you will enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.
slang_text: "Don't go trippin' and think I showed up to trash the Law or the prophets. I didn't come to wreck it -- I came to make it all the way real, like it was always meant to be. For real, I'm tellin' you, till heaven and earth are outta here, not even the tiniest letter or the smallest little pen mark is gonna disappear from the Law till everything goes down like it's supposed to. So whoever breaks even one of these little rules, and teaches other people to do the same, is gonna get called the lamest in the Kingdom of Heaven. But whoever keeps 'em and teaches 'em is gonna get called great in the Kingdom of Heaven. 'Cause I'm tellin' you, unless you're way more righteous than the scribes and Pharisees, there is no way, no how you're gettin' into the Kingdom of Heaven."

web_text:
“You have heard that it was said to the ancient ones, ‘You shall not murder;’
and ‘Whoever murders will be in danger of the judgment.’ But I tell you, that everyone who is angry with his brother without a cause


will be in danger of the judgment; and whoever says to his brother, ‘Raca!’

will be in danger of the council; and whoever says, ‘You fool!’ will be in danger of the fire of Gehenna. “If therefore you are offering your gift at the altar, and there remember that your brother has anything against you, leave your gift there before the altar, and go your way. First be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift.
slang_text: "You've heard it said to the old-timers, 'Don't murder,' and 'Whoever murders is gonna face the judgment.' But I'm tellin' you, anybody who's steamin' mad at their brother for no reason is gonna face that same judgment. And whoever calls their brother a total dipstick is gonna answer to the council. And whoever says 'you fool' is headed for the fire of Gehenna, no joke. So if you're bringin' your offering up to the altar and you suddenly remember your brother's got beef with you, leave your gift right there in front of the altar and bounce. Go patch things up with your brother first, then come back and offer your gift."

web_text:
Agree with your adversary quickly, while you are with him on the way; lest perhaps the prosecutor deliver you to the judge, and the judge deliver you to the officer, and you be cast into prison. Most certainly I tell you, you shall by no means get out of there, until you have paid the last penny. “You have heard that it was said,

‘You shall not commit adultery;’ but I tell you that everyone who gazes at a woman to lust after her has committed adultery with her already in his heart.
slang_text: "Squash it with whoever's got beef with you, quick, while you're still walkin' with 'em -- otherwise the guy might hand you over to the judge, the judge hands you to the officer, and you get chucked in the slammer. For real, I'm tellin' you, there is no way you're bustin' outta there till you've paid back every last penny. You've heard it said, 'Don't commit adultery.' But I'm tellin' you, anybody who checks out a woman and starts lustin' after her has already committed adultery with her right there in his heart."

web_text: If your right eye causes you to stumble, pluck it out and throw it away from you. For it is more profitable for you that one of your members should perish, than for your whole body to be cast into Gehenna. If your right hand causes you to stumble, cut it off, and throw it away from you. For it is more profitable for you that one of your members should perish, than for your whole body to be cast into Gehenna. “It was also said, ‘Whoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a writing of divorce,’ but I tell you that whoever puts away his wife, except for the cause of sexual immorality, makes her an adulteress; and whoever marries her when she is put away commits adultery.
slang_text: "If your right eye's trippin' you up, yank it out and toss it -- it's way better to lose one part of your bod than have your whole self chucked into Gehenna. And if your right hand's trippin' you up, chop it off and toss it -- same deal, better to lose a hand than get your whole body cast into Gehenna. It was also said, 'Whoever divorces his wife, gotta give her divorce papers.' teaching as doctrine rules made by men.’” He summoned the multitude, and said to them,

slang_text:

'cause they're out here teaching man-made rules like it's straight from God.” Then he calls the whole crowd over and goes,

But he answered,
“Every plant which my heavenly Father didn’t plant will be uprooted. Leave them alone. They are blind guides of the blind. If the blind guide the blind, both will fall into a pit.” Peter answered him, “Explain the parable to us.” So Jesus said,
“Do you also still not understand?
slang_text:
But Jesus just answers,
"Any plant my Father in heaven didn't plant is getting yanked outta the ground, no exceptions. Just chill and let 'em be. They're blind dudes leading other blind dudes -- and if a blind guy tries to guide another blind guy, they're both bouncing straight into a ditch." Peter pipes up, "Break that parable down for us." So Jesus goes,
"Seriously, you guys still don't get it?

web_text: Don’t you understand that whatever goes into the mouth passes into the belly, and then out of the body? But the things which proceed out of the mouth come out of the heart, and they defile the man. For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, sexual sins, thefts, false testimony, and blasphemies. These are the things which defile the man; but to eat with unwashed hands doesn’t defile the man.”
slang_text: Don't you get it -- whatever you eat just goes into your gut and comes out the other end eventually, no biggie. But the stuff that comes outta your mouth, that's straight from your heart, and that's what makes you grody. 'Cause outta the heart comes wicked thoughts, murder, cheating, sleazy sex stuff, stealing, lying, and trash-talking God. That's the junk that actually messes you up -- eating with unwashed hands? That's totally nothing.”

web_text:
Jesus went out from there, and withdrew into the region of Tyre and Sidon. Behold, a Canaanite woman came out from those borders, and cried, saying, “Have mercy on me, Lord, you son of David! My daughter is severely possessed by a demon!” But he answered her not a word.
His disciples came and begged him, saying, “Send her away; for she cries after us.” But he answered,
“I wasn’t sent to anyone but the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”
slang_text:
Jesus bounces from there and heads out to the Tyre and Sidon area. And check it -- this Canaanite woman comes running out from that territory, crying out, "Have mercy on me, Lord, Son of David! My daughter's got a demon messing her up something fierce!" But Jesus doesn't say a single word back. Not one.
His disciples come up and beg him, "Just send her off, man, she won't quit hollering after us." But he goes,
"I only got sent for the lost sheep of Israel's house. That's my whole mission.”

web_text:
But she came and worshiped him, saying, “Lord, help me.” But he answered,
“It is not appropriate to take the children’s bread and throw it to the dogs.” But she said, “Yes, Lord, but even the dogs eat the crumbs which fall from their masters’ table.” Then Jesus answered her,
“Woman, great is your faith! Be it done to you even as you desire.” And her daughter was healed from that hour.
slang_text:
But she doesn't give up -- she comes right up and kneels before him, going, "Lord, help me. Please." He answers,
"It's not right to take the kids' bread and toss it to the dogs." And she comes back with, "Fer sure, Lord, but even the dogs get to eat the crumbs that fall off their masters' table." Man, that's some serious faith, and Jesus knows it. He tells her,
"Woman, your faith is straight up bodacious! Let it happen just how you want." And right then, that very hour, her daughter was totally healed.

web_text:
Jesus departed from there, and came near to the sea of Galilee; and he went up into the mountain, and sat there. Great multitudes came to him, having with them the lame, blind, mute, maimed, and many others, and they put them down at his feet. He healed them, so that the multitude wondered when they saw the mute speaking, the injured healed, the lame walking, and the blind seeing—and they glorified the God of Israel. Jesus summoned his disciples and said,
“I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days and have nothing to eat. I don’t want to send them away fasting, or they might faint on the way.”
slang_text:
Jesus splits from there and heads over near the Sea of Galilee, climbs up a mountain, and just chills there. Huge crowds come find him, dragging along the lame, the blind, the mute, the messed-up -- all kinds -- and just dump 'em at his feet. He heals every single one, and the crowd is totally blown away watching the mute talk, the broken get fixed, the lame walk, and the blind see -- everybody's praising the God of Israel, epic stuff. Jesus calls his disciples over and says,
"I feel for this crowd, they've been hanging with me three days straight and got nothing to eat. I'm not sending 'em home hungry, or they'll be fainting out there on the road.”

web_text:
The disciples said to him, “Where should we get so many loaves in a deserted place as to satisfy so great a multitude?” Jesus said to them,
“How many loaves do you have?”
They said, “Seven, and a few small fish.” He commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground; and he took the seven loaves and the fish. He gave thanks and broke them, and gave to the disciples, and the disciples to the multitudes.
slang_text:
The disciples go, "Where are we even gonna find enough bread out here in the middle of nowhere to feed a crowd this bodacious?" Jesus asks them,

They say, "Seven, plus a few little fish." So he tells the whole crowd to sit down on the ground, takes the seven loaves and the fish, says a prayer of thanks, breaks it all up, and hands it to the disciples, and the disciples pass it out to everybody.

web_text: They all ate, and were filled. They took up seven baskets full of the broken pieces that were left over. Those who ate were four thousand men, besides women and children. Then he sent away the multitudes, got into the boat, and came into the borders of Magdala.
slang_text: Everybody ate till they were stuffed, no lie. They picked up seven whole baskets of leftover pieces after. And get this -- four thousand dudes ate, not even counting women and kids. Totally epic. Then Jesus sends the crowds home, hops in the boat, and cruises over to the Magdala area.

web_text:
The Pharisees and Sadducees came, and testing him, asked him to show them a sign from heaven. But he answered them,
“When it is evening, you say, ‘It will be fair weather, for the sky is red.’ In the morning, ‘It will be foul weather today, for the sky is red and threatening.’ Hypocrites! You know how to discern the appearance of the sky, but you can’t discern the signs of the times! An evil and adulterous generation seeks after a sign, and there will be no sign given to it, except the sign of the prophet Jonah.”
He left them, and departed.
slang_text: So these Pharisee and Sadducee dudes roll up trying to test him, all "show us a sign from the sky or somethin'." Jesus just shakes his head. "Come on, when the sky's red at night y'all are like, 'sweet, fair weather tomorrow.' Red and gloomy in the morning, you're like, 'bogus, storm's comin'.' You can read the sky like a champ, but you totally can't read the times we're livin' in! This whole evil, cheatin' generation wants a sign, and I got no duh, they ain't gettin' one -- 'cept the sign of Jonah." Then he just bounced, left 'em standin' there.

web_text:
The disciples came to the other side and had forgotten to take bread. Jesus said to them,
“Take heed and beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.” They reasoned among themselves, saying, “We brought no bread.” Jesus, perceiving it, said,
“Why do you reason among yourselves, you of little faith, ‘because you have brought no bread?’
slang_text: The disciples cross over to the other side and totally spaced on grabbing bread. Jesus goes, "Watch out, steer clear of that yeast from the Pharisees and Sadducees." They're huddled up whisperin', "Oh man, we forgot the bread." Jesus catches on and's like, "Why you dudes trippin' and whisperin' about no bread, ya little-faith spazoids?

web_text: Don’t you yet perceive, neither remember the five loaves for the five thousand, and how many baskets you took up? But beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.” Then they understood that he didn’t tell them to beware of the yeast of bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
slang_text: Don't you dudes remember yet -- the five loaves for five thousand people, how many baskets you hauled away? Or the seven loaves for four thousand, how many baskets that time? How do you not get it that I wasn't even talkin' about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees, for real." Then it clicked -- he wasn't dissin' actual bread yeast, he meant the bogus teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

web_text:

slang_text: Now Jesus and the crew get to the Caesarea Philippi turf, and he asks 'em, "Who do people say I am, the Son of Man?" web_text:
Jesus answered him,

and on this rock

I will build my assembly, and the gates of Hades

web_text:
From that time, Jesus began to show his disciples that he must go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and the third day be raised up. slang_text: 'Cause whoever's tryin' to save their own life's gonna lose it, and whoever loses their life for my sake, they're gonna find it, fer sure. But this kind doesn’t go out except by prayer and fasting.” While they were staying in Galilee, Jesus said to them,

Grab that and hand it over for both of us, easy as that."

“Most certainly I tell you, unless you turn, and become as little children, you will in no way enter into the Kingdom of Heaven. Whoever therefore humbles himself as this little child, the same is the greatest in the Kingdom of Heaven.
slang_text: Right about then the disciples rolled up on Jesus, going, "Yo, who's actually the biggest deal in the Kingdom of Heaven?" Jesus called some little kid over, stood him right in the middle of the crew, and said, "For real, I'm telling you, unless you do a total one-eighty and get as chill and humble as this little kid, there's no way, no how, you're getting into the Kingdom of Heaven. So whoever humbles himself down like this kid here, that's the one who's actually the biggest deal in the Kingdom of Heaven.

web_text: Whoever receives one such little child in my name receives me, but whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble, it would be better for him that a huge millstone should be hung around his neck, and that he should be sunk in the depths of the sea. “Woe to the world because of occasions of stumbling! For it must be that the occasions come, but woe to that person through whom the occasion comes! If your hand or your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off, and cast it from you. of fire. See that you don’t despise one of these little ones, for I tell you that in heaven their angels always see the face of my Father who is in heaven. slang_text: So once the crowd's gathered, Pilate asks them, "Who do you want me to release -- Barabbas, or Jesus, the one called Christ?" I had a rough dream about him last night, real rough." But the chief priests and elders work the crowd, talking them into asking for Barabbas and doing away with Jesus.

web_text:
But the governor answered them, “Which of the two do you want me to release to you?”
They said, “Barabbas!” Pilate said to them, “What then shall I do to Jesus, who is called Christ?”
They all said to him, “Let him be crucified!” But the governor said, “Why? What evil has he done?”
But they cried out exceedingly, saying, “Let him be crucified!” So when Pilate saw that nothing was being gained, but rather that a disturbance was starting, he took water, and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, “I am innocent of the blood of this righteous person. You see to it.”
slang_text:
The governor asks them again, "Which of the two you want me to release?"
"Barabbas!" they yell. Pilate says, "So what do I do with Jesus, the one called Christ?"
They all shout back, "Crucify him!" Pilate says, "Why, though? What's he done wrong?"
But they just cry out even louder, "Crucify him!" So when Pilate sees he's getting nowhere and a riot's about to break loose, he takes water and washes his hands in front of the whole crowd, saying, "I'm innocent of this righteous man's blood. This one's on you."

web_text: All the people answered, “May his blood be on us, and on our children!” Then he released to them Barabbas, but Jesus he flogged and delivered to be crucified. Then the governor’s soldiers took Jesus into the Praetorium, and gathered the whole garrison together against him. They stripped him, and put a scarlet robe on him.
slang_text: And all the people answer back, "Let his blood be on us, and on our kids too." So Pilate lets Barabbas go free, but has Jesus flogged and handed over to be crucified. Then the governor's soldiers drag Jesus into the Praetorium and call the whole garrison together against him. They strip him down and throw a scarlet robe on him.

web_text: They braided a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and a reed in his right hand; and they kneeled down before him, and mocked him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!” They spat on him, and took the reed and struck him on the head. When they had mocked him, they took the robe off him, and put his clothes on him, and led him away to crucify him. As they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name, and they compelled him to go with them, that he might carry his cross.
slang_text: They braid up a crown of thorns and jam it on his head, stick a reed in his right hand, then kneel down in front of him mocking him, saying, "Hail, King of the Jews!" They spit on him, grab the reed, and beat him on the head with it. When they're done mocking him, they pull the robe off, put his own clothes back on him, and lead him off to be crucified. On the way out, they grab a man from Cyrene, name of Simon, and force him to carry the cross.

web_text: When they came to a place called “Golgotha”, that is to say, “The place of a skull,” they gave him sour wine to drink mixed with gall. When he had tasted it, he would not drink. When they had crucified him, they divided his clothing among them, casting lots, and they sat and watched him there.
slang_text: When they get to a place called Golgotha -- "The Place of a Skull" -- they give him sour wine mixed with gall to drink. He tastes it and won't drink it. Once they've crucified him, they split up his clothes among themselves, casting lots for them, then sit down and keep watch over him there.

web_text: They set up over his head the accusation against him written, “THIS IS JESUS, THE KING OF THE JEWS.” Then there were two robbers crucified with him, one on his right hand and one on the left. Those who passed by blasphemed him, wagging their heads, and saying, “You who destroy the temple, and build it in three days, save yourself! If you are the Son of God, come down from the cross!”
slang_text: Over his head they put up the charge against him, written out: "THIS IS JESUS, THE KING OF THE JEWS." Two robbers get crucified alongside him, one on his right and one on his left. People walking by hurl insults at him, shaking their heads, saying, "So you're the one who was gonna tear down the temple and rebuild it in three days -- save yourself! If you're really the Son of God, come on down off that cross!"

web_text: Likewise the chief priests also mocking, with the scribes, the Pharisees, and the elders, said, “He saved others, but he can’t save himself. If he is the King of Israel, let him come down from the cross now, and we will believe in him. He trusts in God. Let God deliver him now, if he wants him; for he said, ‘I am the Son of God.’” The robbers also who were crucified with him cast on him the same reproach.
slang_text: Same deal with the chief priests, mocking right along with the scribes, Pharisees, and elders: "He saved others, but he can't even save himself. If he's really the King of Israel, let him climb down off that cross right now and we'll believe in him. He's put his trust in God -- so let God rescue him now, if God even wants him, 'cause he said, 'I'm the Son of God.'" Even the robbers hanging next to him throw the same insults at him.

web_text:
Now from the sixth hour there was darkness over all the land until the ninth hour. About the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying,
“Eli, Eli, lima
sabachthani?”
 That is,
“My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?” Some of them who stood there, when they heard it, said, “This man is calling Elijah.” Immediately one of them ran, and took a sponge, and filled it with vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him a drink.
slang_text:
From noon on, darkness falls over the whole land, all the way till three in the afternoon. Around three, Jesus cries out loud,
"Eli, Eli, lima sabachthani?"
which means,
"My God, my God, why have you left me?" Some of the people standing there hear it and say, "This guy's calling for Elijah." One of them runs off right away, grabs a sponge, soaks it in vinegar, sticks it on a reed, and holds it up for him to drink.

web_text: The rest said, “Let him be. Let’s see whether Elijah comes to save him.” Jesus cried again with a loud voice, and yielded up his spirit. Behold, the veil of the temple was torn in two from the top to the bottom. The earth quaked and the rocks were split. The tombs were opened, and many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised;
slang_text: But the rest say, "Leave him alone. Let's see if Elijah shows up to save him." Jesus cries out loud one more time and gives up his spirit. And right then, the veil of the temple rips in two, top to bottom. The ground shakes and the rocks split open. Tombs crack open, and a bunch of saints who'd died get raised up;

web_text: and coming out of the tombs after his resurrection, they entered into the holy city and appeared to many. Now the centurion, and those who were with him watching Jesus, when they saw the earthquake, and the things that were done, feared exceedingly, saying, “Truly this was the Son of God.” Many women were there watching from afar, who had followed Jesus from Galilee, serving him. Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
slang_text: and coming out of their tombs after his resurrection, they go into the holy city and show up in front of a lot of people. Now the centurion and the ones standing guard with him watching Jesus -- when they see the earthquake and everything else that happened, they get scared out of their minds, saying, "This was really the Son of God." Plenty of women were there too, watching from a distance, the ones who'd followed Jesus from Galilee and looked after him. Mary Magdalene was among them, along with Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of Zebedee's sons.

web_text: When evening had come, a rich man from Arimathaea, named Joseph, who himself was also Jesus’ disciple came. This man went to Pilate, and asked for Jesus’ body. Then Pilate commanded the body to be given up. Joseph took the body, and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth, and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had cut out in the rock, and he rolled a great stone against the door of the tomb, and departed.
slang_text: When evening comes, a rich man from Arimathaea named Joseph -- one of Jesus's own disciples -- goes to Pilate and asks for Jesus's body. Pilate gives the order to hand it over. Joseph takes the body, wraps it up in a clean linen cloth, and lays it in his own brand-new tomb, cut right out of the rock. Then he rolls a huge stone across the entrance and goes on his way.

web_text: Mary Magdalene was there, and the other Mary, sitting opposite the tomb. Now on the next day, which was the day after the Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees were gathered together to Pilate, saying, “Sir, we remember what that deceiver said while he was still alive: ‘After three days I will rise again.’ Command therefore that the tomb be made secure until the third day, lest perhaps his disciples come at night and steal him away, and tell the people, ‘He is risen from the dead;’ and the last deception will be worse than the first.”
slang_text: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there too, sitting across from the tomb. The next day -- the day after Preparation Day -- the chief priests and Pharisees go to Pilate together and say, "Sir, we remember that con man saying while he was still alive, 'After three days I'll rise again.' So give the order to lock down that tomb till the third day, or else his disciples might sneak in at night and steal the body, then tell everybody, 'He's risen from the dead' -- and that last lie would be worse than the first one."

web_text: Pilate said to them, “You have a guard. Go, make it as secure as you can.” So they went with the guard and made the tomb secure, sealing the stone.
slang_text: Pilate tells them, "You've got a guard. Go make it as secure as you can." So they go with the guard, lock the tomb down tight, and seal up the stone.

web_text: Now after the Sabbath, as it began to dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to see the tomb. Behold, there was a great earthquake, for an angel of the Lord descended from the sky, and came and rolled away the stone from the door, and sat on it. His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing white as snow. For fear of him, the guards shook, and became like dead men.
slang_text: So the Sabbath's done, and right at the crack of dawn Sunday, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary bounce on over to check out the tomb. And oh snap -- the ground starts shaking like a hella gnarly earthquake, 'cause an angel of the Lord comes cruising down outta the sky, rolls that stone away from the door like it's nothing, and just plops down on it, cool as you please. Dude was straight-up psychedelic to look at -- lit up like lightning, threads white as fresh snow. The guards took one look and totally lost it, shaking like spazzes, dropping like they were dead men.

web_text: The angel answered the women, “Don’t be afraid, for I know that you seek Jesus, who has been crucified. He is not here, for he has risen, just like he said. Come, see the place where the Lord was lying. Go quickly and tell his disciples, ‘He has risen from the dead, and behold, he goes before you into Galilee; there you will see him.’ Behold, I have told you.” They departed quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, and ran to bring his disciples word.
slang_text: The angel goes to the women, "Chill, don't have a cow, I know you're looking for Jesus, the one they nailed up. Well guess what -- he ain't here, 'cause he's risen, just like he totally called it. C'mere, check out the spot where the Lord was laid out. Now bounce, quick, and tell his disciples, 'He's risen from the dead, and get this -- he's headed to Galilee ahead of you, and that's where you'll catch him.' There, I told you, straight up." So they book it outta the tomb, freaked out but totally stoked, and run like the wind to spill the news to the disciples.

web_text:
As they went to tell his disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying,
“Rejoice!”
They came and took hold of his feet, and worshiped him. Then Jesus said to them,
“Don’t be afraid. Go tell my brothers

that they should go into Galilee, and there they will see me.” Now while they were going, behold, some of the guards came into the city, and told the chief priests all the things that had happened. When they were assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave a large amount of silver to the soldiers,
slang_text:
As they're heading out to spill the news, boom -- Jesus himself meets 'em on the road and goes,

"Cowabunga!"

They rush him and grab his feet, worshiping him like a boss. Then Jesus goes to 'em,

"Chill, don't be scared. Go tell my brothers

they need to head to Galilee, and that's where they'll see me." Meanwhile, while the women are booking it, check it -- some of the guards sneak into the city and spill everything that went down to the chief priests. So the priests huddle up with the elders, cook up a plan, and hand over a fat wad of silver to the soldiers,

web_text: saying, “Say that his disciples came by night, and stole him away while we slept. If this comes to the governor’s ears, we will persuade him and make you free of worry.” So they took the money and did as they were told. This saying was spread abroad among the Jews, and continues until today. But the eleven disciples went into Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus had sent them.
slang_text: saying, "Tell everybody his disciples snuck in at night and jacked the body while you were straight-up zonked out. And if that bogus story gets back to the governor, don't sweat it, we'll smooth talk him and keep you outta trouble." So the soldiers pocket the cash and run with the cover story like they're told, no duh. That tale got spread all over among the Jews and it's still floating around today, for real. Meanwhile the eleven disciples bounce on out to Galilee, to that mountain where Jesus told 'em to meet up.

web_text:
When they saw him, they bowed down to him, but some doubted. Jesus came to them and spoke to them, saying,
“All authority has been given to me in heaven and on earth. Go
and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, teaching them to observe all things that I commanded you. Behold, I am with you always, even to the end of the age.” Amen.
slang_text:
When they see him, they bow down, totally in awe, though a few of 'em are still trippin', not sure it's really him. Jesus rolls up to 'em and lays it down,

"All authority, in heaven and on earth, has been handed to me, righteous and total. So go

and make disciples outta all the nations, dunking 'em in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit, teaching 'em to keep everything I laid on you. And check it -- I'm with you always, no matter what, all the way to the end of the age." Amen, dudes.
